Channel.title: Channel
Channel.description: Represents a YouTube channel managed by the AI Lo-Fi Channel Factory, defining its niche and content strategy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Channel entity.

name.description: The public name of the YouTube channel.

niche.description: The specific micro-niche or thematic focus of the channel (e.g., 'Cozy Study Lo-Fi', 'Halloween Lo-Fi').

uploadFrequency.description: The typical frequency at which new content is uploaded to the channel (e.g., 'Daily', 'Weekly', 'Bi-Weekly').

youtubeChannelId.description: The unique ID assigned by YouTube to this channel, used for API interactions.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the channel record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the channel record was updated.

ContentProject.title: ContentProject
ContentProject.description: Manages a collection of related content items (videos, shorts) for a specific channel, forming a content 'album' or campaign.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentProject entity.

name.description: A user-defined name for the content project (e.g., 'Halloween Lofi Beats', 'Winter Chillhop Mix').

description.description: A detailed description or brief for the content project.

channelId.description: Reference to the Channel entity this project belongs to. (Relationship: Channel 1:N ContentProject)

status.description: The current overall status of the content project (e.g., 'Draft', 'Active', 'Completed', 'Archived').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content project record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the content project record was updated.

ContentItem.title: ContentItem
ContentItem.description: Represents a single piece of generated content (e.g., a long-form video, YouTube Short, or live stream segment) ready for publication.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContentItem entity.

contentProjectId.description: Reference to the ContentProject this item belongs to. (Relationship: ContentProject 1:N ContentItem)

type.description: The type of content this item represents (e.g., 'LongFormVideo', 'Short', 'LiveStream').

title.description: The AI-generated or user-defined title for the YouTube video/short.

description.description: The AI-generated or user-defined description for the YouTube video/short, including SEO metadata.

tags.description: An array of AI-generated or user-defined tags/hashtags for YouTube SEO.

status.description: The current production status of the content item (e.g., 'Idea', 'MusicGenerated', 'VisualsGenerated', 'Assembled', 'AwaitingReview', 'Approved', 'Scheduled', 'Uploaded', 'Error').

durationMinutes.description: The planned or actual duration of the content item in minutes.

musicPrompt.description: The AI prompt used to generate the music for this content item.

visualsPrompt.description: The AI prompt used to generate the visuals/artwork for this content item.

musicAssetUrl.description: URL to the generated instrumental Lo-Fi audio track asset.

visualsAssetUrl.description: URL to the generated looping visual/animation asset.

videoFileUrl.description: URL to the final assembled and rendered video file.

thumbnailFileUrl.description: URL to the generated thumbnail image for the YouTube video.

youtubeVideoId.description: The unique ID assigned by YouTube after the video has been successfully uploaded.

scheduledPublishAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content item is scheduled to be published on YouTube.

publishedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content item was actually published on YouTube.

isApprovedForPublish.description: A flag indicating whether the content item has received user approval for final publication.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the content item record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the content item record was updated.

Beat.title: Beat
Beat.description: Represents an individual AI-generated beat that can be monetized through external platforms.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Beat entity.

contentItemId.description: Reference to the ContentItem where this beat is primarily featured or originated from. (Relationship: ContentItem 1:N Beat)

title.description: The title or name of the beat.

audioUrl.description: URL to the standalone audio file of the beat.

bpm.description: Beats per minute of the track.

key.description: The musical key of the beat (e.g., 'C minor', 'F# major').

genre.description: The genre of the beat (e.g., 'Lo-Fi Hip Hop', 'Chillhop', 'Ambient Lo-Fi').

licensingOptions.description: An array of available licensing options for this beat (e.g., 'MP3 Lease', 'WAV Lease', 'Exclusive').

externalListingUrl.description: URL to the external platform (e.g., BeatStars, Gumroad) where this beat is listed for sale.

priceUSD.description: The base price of the beat in USD for standard licensing.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the beat record was created.

ProductionJob.title: ProductionJob
ProductionJob.description: Tracks the execution instance of an automated content production task or workflow step for a specific content item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProductionJob entity.

contentItemId.description: Reference to the ContentItem this job is processing. (Relationship: ContentItem 1:N ProductionJob)

jobType.description: The type of production task being executed (e.g., 'GenerateMusic', 'GenerateVisuals', 'AssembleVideo', 'PublishVideo', 'FetchAnalytics').

status.description: The current status of this specific job instance (e.g., 'Queued', 'Running', 'Completed', 'Failed', 'Cancelled').

startedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the production job began execution.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the production job finished execution, whether successfully or with an error.

errorMessage.description: Detailed error message if the production job failed.

logOutputUrl.description: URL to detailed logs or output from the production job.

workerInstanceId.description: Identifier of the worker or cloud function instance that executed this job.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the production job record was created (e.g., when it was queued).

AnalyticsSnapshot.title: AnalyticsSnapshot
AnalyticsSnapshot.description: Records a snapshot of performance metrics for a specific content item at a given point in time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AnalyticsSnapshot entity.

contentItemId.description: Reference to the ContentItem this analytics data pertains to. (Relationship: ContentItem 1:N AnalyticsSnapshot)

snapshotDate.description: The date for which this analytics snapshot was captured.

views.description: Total views recorded for the content item up to the snapshot date.

ctr.description: Click-through rate for the content item at the snapshot date (as a percentage).

watchTimeHours.description: Total watch time in hours for the content item up to the snapshot date.

averageViewDurationSeconds.description: Average view duration in seconds for the content item at the snapshot date.

rpm.description: Revenue per mille (per 1000 views) for the content item.

beatSalesCount.description: Number of beat sales attributed to this specific content item up to the snapshot date.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this analytics snapshot record was created in the system.

definition.description: Root collection for all user-specific data. While 'User' is an implicit entity, this collection holds core user profiles and acts as the secure entry point for all owned content.
Stores details for each YouTube channel managed by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the channel.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Channel entity.

definition.description: Manages collections of related content items for a specific channel. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'channelId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the content project.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel.

params.name: contentProjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ContentProject entity.

definition.description: Represents a single piece of generated content (e.g., video, short). Includes denormalized 'ownerId', 'channelId', and 'contentProjectId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the content item.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel.

params.name: contentProjectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content project.

params.name: contentItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ContentItem entity.

definition.description: Stores details for individual AI-generated beats. Includes denormalized 'ownerId', 'channelId', 'contentProjectId', and 'contentItemId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the beat.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel.

params.name: contentProjectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content project.

params.name: contentItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content item.

params.name: beatId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Beat entity.

definition.description: Tracks the execution instances of automated content production tasks for a content item. Includes denormalized 'ownerId', 'channelId', 'contentProjectId', and 'contentItemId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the production job.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel.

params.name: contentProjectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content project.

params.name: contentItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content item.

params.name: productionJobId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ProductionJob entity.

definition.description: Records performance metrics for a content item at a given time. Includes denormalized 'ownerId', 'channelId', 'contentProjectId', and 'contentItemId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the analytics snapshot.

params.name: channelId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent channel.

params.name: contentProjectId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content project.

params.name: contentItemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent content item.

params.name: analyticsSnapshotId
params.description: The unique identifier for the AnalyticsSnapshot entity.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs)** by leveraging a deeply nested, path-based ownership model combined with comprehensive denormalization of authorization context. All user-specific data is rooted under a dedicated `/users/{userId}` collection, ensuring strict data segregation per user.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To achieve this, every document in the hierarchy (Channel, ContentProject, ContentItem, Beat, ProductionJob, AnalyticsSnapshot) will include a denormalized `ownerId` field, which stores the `uid` of the user who owns the top-level channel. Additionally, documents at lower levels will denormalize the IDs of their direct parent collections (e.g., `ContentItem` documents will include `channelId` and `contentProjectId`). This design eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent documents for authorization. For instance, a rule for a `ContentItem` can directly verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` and `resource.data.contentProjectId == contentProjectId` (from the path), ensuring atomic, efficient, and easily debuggable authorization checks.

**Queryable Authorization Patterns (QAPs):** The hierarchical path structure (`/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/...`) inherently supports secure `list` operations. When querying for data, users are automatically restricted to their own branch of the data (`/users/{request.auth.uid}/...`). For example, listing all channels for a user simply involves querying the `channels` subcollection under their `userId` path. Furthermore, the denormalized `ownerId` in every document enables secure collection group queries (if needed) across deeply nested collections, allowing `list` operations to be filtered securely by `ownerId == request.auth.uid` without compromising data integrity or security. This prevents rules from acting as filters, ensuring that only authorized data is ever returned by a query.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection (`channels`, `contentProjects`, `contentItems`, etc.) at its respective level is designed to hold documents with a homogeneous security posture. All documents within a given subcollection will belong to the same `ownerId` and associated parent IDs, simplifying security rules immensely by moving complexity from rule logic to the data structure itself.

**Access Modeling:** The design consistently uses `Path-Based Ownership` for all user-owned data, continuing the hierarchy for nested `1:N` relationships. This aligns with the 'User 1:N Channel', 'Channel 1:N ContentProject', and subsequent relationships.

**Original Schema Mapping:**
*   `videos` entity from user request is mapped to `ContentItem` under `/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/contentProjects/{contentProjectId}/contentItems/{contentItemId}`.
*   `channels` entity maps directly to `Channel` under `/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}`.
*   `analytics` entity maps to `AnalyticsSnapshot` under `/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/contentProjects/{contentProjectId}/contentItems/{contentItemId}/analyticsSnapshots/{analyticsSnapshotId}`.
*   `tasks` entity maps to `ProductionJob` under `/users/{userId}/channels/{channelId}/contentProjects/{contentProjectId}/contentItems/{contentItemId}/productionJobs/{productionJobId}`.

This structure ensures a secure, scalable, and debuggable foundation for the AI Lo-Fi Channel Factory, allowing for robust rule sets and efficient data access patterns.
